Tragacanth is a type of succulent plant that produces a unique gum or resin, known as tragacanth gum, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Tragacanth include its ability to soothe digestive issues, act as a natural laxative, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include tragacanth gum, which is composed of arabinose, galactose, and glucuronic acid, as well as other polysaccharides and phenolic compounds. The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacanth include topical ointments, creams, and suppositories, as well as oral capsules and teas, often used to treat digestive issues and promote wound healing.
